opus_reference_baseline.notes: claude-opus-4-7: sampling params deprecated, omit entirely

meeting_minutes_extraction.purpose: Full 21-type meeting minutes extraction — requires reliable instruction-following at 6500-token prompt complexity. Haiku ceiling exceeded at 34-chunk full transcript scale.
meeting_minutes_extraction.notes: Upgrade from haiku-4-5 (P8-A). Route high-volume STANDARD types back to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 once P8-A per-type routing splits the single full-payload pass into per-tier model calls — until then ONE Sonnet pass produces the whole 21-type payload. max_tokens is the transport budget for that single whole-transcript response, NOT an eval gate: a full 34-chunk Sonnet extraction emits the entire payload (decisions/action_items/open_questions + ~20 structured arrays + grounding) in one response and needs well past 8192 output tokens; 16384 is the proven non-truncating value (same root cause documented in workflows/llm_client.py::_MAX_TOKENS and create_opus_reference_baselines._OPUS_MAX_TOKENS). A still-over-budget or malformed response is STILL blocked fail-closed by the unchanged strict-schema eval — nothing is weakened.

note: No hardcoded model strings anywhere in code. All model resolution must use the registry. Update this file to migrate model versions.
